subject: Physics
title: The EPR Paradox
description: A 60-second lesson on The EPR Paradox.

hook.text: In 1935, Einstein tried to kill quantum mechanics with a thought experiment. It backfired spectacularly.

buildup.text: Einstein, Podolsky, and Rosen argued that if entanglement is real, quantum mechanics must be incomplete.

discovery.text: They said hidden variables must predetermine outcomes. Quantum randomness was just our ignorance.

twist.text: Decades later, experiments proved Einstein wrong. There are no hidden variables. Randomness is real.

climax.text: The EPR paper, meant to destroy quantum mechanics, instead became one of its most important foundations.

punchline.text: Einstein tried to break quantum physics. He ended up proving it.

quiz.question: What did the EPR paper argue about quantum mechanics?
options: That it was incomplete and hidden variables must exist
